Text[0]: アイテムを使ってみよう_パッド・1
If you are attacked while on chocoback, your steed may buck you and run off to safety. You may only summon him again once the immediate threat has been neutralized.

The higher your <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>perception<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> rating, the better chance you have of obtaining high-quality (<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>HQ<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>) items while gathering.

(Some items do not have an HQ counterpart.)

You can use the Disciple of the Land action <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Preparation<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> to confirm how high your perception rating must be before you are able to obtain HQ items. The percentage displayed in the gathering interface indicates your chance of success.

Using certain actions or meeting certain attribute conditions can increase your chances of finding HQ items, but only if your perception rating is sufficient enough to obtain HQ items in the first place.

The one drawback to this command is that it reduces movement speed by almost half, so when there are no threats nearby, it is recommended that you cancel the status by selecting the Stealth icon on your <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>hotbar<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> once again.

Stealth cannot be used if you are being attacked by an enemy. Also, enemies with levels significantly higher than yours may be able to detect you, regardless of whether or not you have Stealth activated.

The Disciple of the Land action <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Stealth<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> allows you to conceal yourself from enemies so that you may log, mine, or fish without fear of being attacked.

Upon accessing certain high-level gathering points, you may find that items are <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>out of reach<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. To find out what these items are, you will have to spend your turns clearing away excess growth or debris.
If you are lucky, you may find a rare item. If not, you may find the slot barren. Either way, you will not know until the debris is removed.

The higher your <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>gathering<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> rating, the more debris you will clear with each attempt. There are also high-level actions such as <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Toil of the Pioneer<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> and <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Toil of the Mountaineer<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> that, when used, instantly clear away all debris at a gathering point, allowing you to see where the items are located without having to use up your attempts.

<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Instanced raids<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> are party-based content which take place in closed environments such as dungeons. The term “instanced” refers to the fact that these areas are reserved for a single group of players, meaning that you will not encounter any others during your time in them.

Parties must rely on their own wits and strength to conquer the many challenges that await them, such as deadly traps, complex mazes, and powerful bosses.

To add to the challenge, instanced raids have numerous requirements such as time limits, level restrictions, and limitations of changing classes or jobs while within.

Finally, raids can be joined either by forming a party and speaking with the NPC at the entrance, or by using the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Duty Finder<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> to automatically pair you with other adventurers around the world with the same ambitions.

By unlocking the Duty Finder, you can now undertake multiplayer duties without having to form a party on your own.

Press the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>U<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> key or select <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Duty Finder<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> under <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Duty<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> from the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>main menu<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> to bring up the finder interface.

By unlocking the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Duty Finder<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, you can now undertake multiplayer duties without having to form a party on your own.

Select <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Duty Finder<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> under <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Duty<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> from the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>main menu<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> to bring up the finder interface.

First, highlight a duty from the list and press the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Join<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> button to begin the matching process. A <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>padlock icon<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> indicates duty that cannot be accessed until you have achieved certain requirements such as level or quest completion.

Once you have registered, you can view the duty's particulars, including party and server status, by selecting <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Duty Finder<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> from the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>duty list<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

When your party has been successfully formed, a confirmation prompt will appear.

Remember, it is good practice to work out a battle plan with your party before rushing headlong into an instance.

Outfitting your level 50 retainer with the proper gear will increase her chances of finding rare items while on exploration ventures. After assigning your retainer a quick exploration venture, keep an eye on your log window for a message telling you whether or not her current equipment is sufficient.

Some NPCs will seek to barter with you for your collectables, but will require that the items are of a certain collectability. Confirm these numbers before gathering or crafting a collectable, lest you procure an item no one wants.

Using the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Collectable Synthesis<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> command will grant you the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Collectable Synthesis<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> status. While under the effect of this status, you will have the option to “finish” certain items as collectables after the final step of successful synthesis.

Increasing an item's quality through synthesis will also increase its <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>collectability<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. If, by the final step, the collectability achieved is not at a level deemed acceptable by the NPC seeking the item, it is best to complete the synthesis normally so that the item might be sold or used.

While under the effect of the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Collector's Glove<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> status, you will be given the option to gather items as <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>collectables<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

Items are now suitable for gathering as <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>collectables<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. Before gathering the item, use the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Appraisal<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> actions to increase its <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>rarity<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>, and in turn, its <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>collectability<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>.

While appraisal increases rarity, it also increases an item's <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>wear<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground>. If wear increases beyond an item's limit, gathering rate will drop significantly.

<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Duty actions<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> are special actions that are available only during the course of specific quests or battles. Duty actions are used by pressing <CommandIcon(17)/> while holding down both <CommandIcon(14)/> and <CommandIcon(15)/>.

<UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Duty actions<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> are special actions that are available only during the course of specific quests or battles. Duty actions are used by pressing <Gui(17)/> while holding down both <CommandIcon(14)/> and <CommandIcon(15)/>.

When bubbles appear within the teeming waters, it means a fish has approached. Use the <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>Gig<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> command at this time to spear the creature and add it to your inventory.

There are several types of <UIForeground>F201F4</UIForeground><UIGlow>F201F5</UIGlow>gig heads<UIGlow>01</UIGlow><UIForeground>01</UIForeground> each specialized for catching different kinds of fish. If it looks like you are not having luck with your current head, change it! All gigs come complete with a set of interchangeable heads.
